# Project Context: youtube-dj-webcam-streams

<!-- Auto-generated by /cortex-spec from spec artifacts. -->
<!-- All agents reference this file for project-level conventions. -->

## Tech Stack

- **OBS Studio** (v30+) — headless video compositing with obs-websocket v5
- **Xvfb** — virtual framebuffer for headless OBS on Linux
- **Liquidsoap** (v2.2+) — audio playout, crossfading, playlist management, telnet control
- **Redis** (v7+) — Pub/Sub command bus for decoupled pipeline control
- **FFmpeg** (v6+) — internal compositing engine (used by OBS), fallback prototype pipeline
- **Python** (3.11+) — chatbot, controllers, health monitors, API integrations
- **systemd** — process management, watchdog, service orchestration

## Conventions

- Python source code in `src/`
- Configuration files in `config/`
- Systemd unit files and startup scripts in `scripts/`
- All pipeline control goes through Redis Pub/Sub — no direct chatbot-to-OBS/Liquidsoap calls
- Redis channels: `stream:audio`, `stream:video`, `stream:layout`
- Command messages are JSON: `{"action": "...", "params": {...}, "source": "chatbot|health_monitor|auto"}`

## Architecture Rules

- OBS handles all video compositing — Liquidsoap handles all audio playout. No overlap.
- All runtime control flows through Redis Pub/Sub. Controllers subscribe to channels and translate to OBS WebSocket / Liquidsoap telnet calls.
- Webcam feeds sourced primarily from Windy Webcams API (HLS). DOT JPEG feeds as supplementary.
- Music: owner's DJ library (commercial tracks, accept Content ID claims). Mubert API for autonomous/unattended mode only.
- Every service runs as a systemd unit with watchdog. No manual process management.
- YouTube 12-hour stream limit is a hard constraint — auto-restart logic is mandatory.
- Prefer low-resolution and scenic/traffic webcam feeds to minimize GDPR exposure from EU sources.

## Write Boundaries

- `src/` — Python source code
- `config/` — configuration files
- `scripts/` — systemd units, operational scripts
- `tests/` — test files
- `docs/` — documentation and runbook
